examples.title: Reading Command Output with popen/pclose
examples.description: Program to execute 'ls -l' and read its output using popen() and pclose()
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates the basic usage of popen() in 'r' mode to read command output. It opens a pipe to 'ls -l', reads each line using fgets(), prints the output, and uses pclose() to close the pipe and retrieve the command's exit status using WEXITSTATUS macro.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of output lines
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for buffer, O(n) for total output stored

examples.title: Writing to Command with popen/pclose
examples.description: Program to send input to a command (wc -l to count lines) using popen() and pclose()
examples.explanation: This demonstrates popen() in 'w' mode to send data to a command. The program writes three lines to 'wc -l' which counts the lines and outputs '3'. The pclose() is called to close the pipe and wait for the command to complete, collecting its exit status.

examples.title: Error Handling with pclose
examples.description: Demonstrates proper error handling for pclose() return value
examples.explanation: This code shows comprehensive error handling for both popen() and pclose(). It handles the case when pclose() returns -1 (error), and also uses WIFEXITED and WIFSIGNALED to properly interpret the exit status, distinguishing between normal exit and signal termination.

examples.title: popen/pclose Algorithm Summary
examples.description: High-level algorithm showing the complete popen and pclose workflow
examples.code:
ALGORITHM popen_and_pclose(command, mode)
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Create pipe
 CREATE pipe with pipe() system call
 
 // Step 2: Fork child process
 pid = fork()
 
 IF pid == 0 THEN // Child process
 CLOSE read end of pipe (if mode = 'w')
 CLOSE write end of pipe (if mode = 'r')
 REDIRECT stdout/stdin to pipe
 EXEC /bin/sh with -c flag to run command
 ELSE // Parent process
 CLOSE write end of pipe (if mode = 'r')
 CLOSE read end of pipe (if mode = 'w')
 RETURN FILE pointer to pipe
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM pclose(pipe_stream)
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Close the pipe stream
 CLOSE(pipe_stream)
 
 // Step 2: Wait for child process
 waitpid(child_pid, &status, 0)
 
 // Step 3: Return status
 RETURN status
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the internal working of popen() and pclose(). popen() creates a pipe, forks a child that executes the command via shell, and returns a FILE pointer. pclose() closes the stream and waits for the child to finish, returning its exit status.
